item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very sweet story. It is a nice change.

One very small nitpick, instead of the Queen saying "Oh, l'anglais?" it should be "Oh, de l'anglais" ("l'anglais" means "the English", as a person, while "de l'anglais" means "English", as a language).

item.title: Re: [[/size]] tag
item.content:encoded: Thank you for getting that for me!

item.title: Re: [[/size]] tag
item.content:encoded: loddite, please do not reply to your own posts, as it builds up spam. If you must, please use the "Edit" option located in the "Options" at the bottom right of your post.

item.title: Re: [[/size]] tag
item.content:encoded: ok i fixed it

item.title: [[/size]] tag
item.content:encoded: for me on mobile there's a bare tag in the last letter

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The character of the Rose Queen really resonated with me in a way I wasn't expecting—I can absolutely relate to the whole "setting myself on fire to keep others warm" mentality. This skip was a punch in the emotions and I applaud you for that.

+1

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: You have my permission to add this crosslink! I asked Nicolini about it, he's on board with it too. :)

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Uncle Nicolini informed me that Commander Hart featured in this article is the same Commander from SCP-3358. A pretty simple link, but given how SCP-3358 is linked out to another Wikiwalk skip, this makes for a nice string to follow. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

MTF Pi-30 agent Craig Hart was the first to locate and interact with SCP-4299. Hart refused SCP-4299's offer as per mission control's orders.

To this:

MTF Pi-30 agent Craig Hart was the first to locate and interact with SCP-4299. Hart refused SCP-4299's offer as per mission control's orders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so very lovely. I've always loved the SCPs that aren't exactly horror, but are instead more wholesome. It's always a nice little break from the monsters that eat people and the prisoners who never see their family..
Also, rose-tinted glasses. Puns!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. it’s really cute.
yee haw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Told me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delicate and lovely. I think the dialogue in the interviews could use a little work, and I do feel this could be construed as an overly glib, trite approach to depression, but I don't feel like that was your intention. A very refreshing article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall great emotional article. Big ol' +1 from me.

Small thing though. A stigma is a negative connotation towards something, or it is the appearance of the wounds of Jesus on a person to signify their blessing by God (like Saint Francis of Assisi). I believe you meant to use the term "stamen", which are the pollen producing sexual organs of a flower. Not all flowers have them 100% of the time, but luckily, roses always have upwards of 5.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's understandable. In the early drafting stages, we actually had Mia as an American woman who flew to France just to check out the garden, the note is the only part that we've left relatively unchanged. In my mind, I like to think that Maria was born from an American father, justifying Mia's ability to speak English. I might write a tale about that some time.

In other words, the letter was meant to add depth to the Queen/Mia and was there to act as an early emotional punch; it was originally the ending part of this when we were drafting. The formatting as well was meant to act as a sort of 'pseudo-picture' as well, which was justified by the 'document attached.' I understand the issue you might have with this, but I thought that it would help the reader put themselves in the shoes of the daughter reading the actual note for the first time.

It's unfortunate that you disliked some aspects of this, and I really hope you give the piece a second chance.

EDIT: In regard of the expected horror twist, I feel that would just be a stain on what we were going for. This was all around made to try and envoke an emotional reaction from the reader.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mixed feelings about this. I like the flower character, her development through the article and the atmosphere created, like the Edith Piaf references. I don't really like the 'wow thanks I'm cured' approach to mental illness (to clarify, I don't think this is an offensive portrayal of mental illness as much as it is a simplistic one which leaves the narrative potential of the subject unexplored) or the letter. I also was expecting this to have a horror twist, which left me feeling a bit unsatisfied, though I don't think that would have improved the article as such.

My dislike of the letter is very pedantic, but stems from it feeling out of place. Assuming this letter was found in the French garden where the SCP was discovered, it's written in English (presumably, without a note it was translated), by someone with an un-French-sounding name and by someone who appears not to be completely familiar with La Vie en Rose, which is like the most famous French song of all time. Of course all of these things are completely possible in a letter written by someone who lives in France, but taken together make the author's non-French voice (presumably) too obvious. I also don't like this trend I've seen of using a cursive font for handwritten letters. No government agency changes their documents to a cursive font to indicate handwriting - they would either just transcribe the letter or take a photograph of it. The alternative, that it's meant to improve immersion for the reader by creating the illusion of handwriting, also makes no sense, as it's clearly a computer generated font.

Novoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The formatting for the letter as well as its contents got me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
really glad you liked it, we spent a lot time trying to make it something emotional

💖

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you're pampering me

I remember when I read the draft it really shattered my night and filled it with heaps of self reflection, remorse, sadness and hope. I woke up different that day. +

💖

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll take care of it shortly. Glad to see you liked it, Zyn!

E- It's been taken care of. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 from me! I remember reading and liking this in the forums; it's good to see it on the mainsite!

Of note, author, please localize your image! If you would like assistance with that, let me know.

item.title: Re: Beautiful.
item.content:encoded:
Beautiful Haiku
Made by a dear friend for us
Thank you, Gabriel Jade.

item.title: Beautiful.
item.content:encoded:
You can give too much.
Love yourself, your dearest friend.
Rose petals cascade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats ya nerds! Glad to finally see it up. This was lovely and I love pink so +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
In endless nights of love,
A great delight that comes about,
The pains and bothers are banished
Happy, happy to die of love.

Recommended Reading Music: La Vie en Rose

Alternative Title 'Je Vois La Vie en Rose'

Enjoyed this SCP? Click here to check out more of my work on my Author Page!

I seriously cannot stress how much I have enjoyed crafting this piece. The Queen is likely among one of the best characters I have ever crafted. I seriously couldn't have done this without my co-author and partner in crime Uncle Nicolini who greatly helped me in creating this when the article was nothing but a garden that made you happy. Check out his author page here.

We went through a lot of ideas for this piece. Originally, this was nothing but an article about a rose garden that would cause people to become extremely happy and eventually undergo a compulsion to return to the garden and explode into rose petals. We scrapped this idea after getting advice from one of our critics. The concept of the Queen came to my mind when I was doing yard work and looked at my rose bush, which honestly is pretty neat when I think about.

+ CRITIQUE ATTRIBUTIONS
- heck

Prototype_Toaster, Veiedhimaedhr, HumidHermit, Zyn, Doctor Cimmerian, Weryllium and those who looked at it in SCPDeclassified.

+ Image Attribution
- heck

Photo is under public domain.
